question: How do I embed Kaltura media in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor?
ground_truth: Open the Rich Content Editor in Canvas LMS, click Embed Kaltura Media, choose media from My Media or a Media Gallery tab, then click Embed next to the item. Use the gear icon before embedding if you need custom player settings.
contexts:
Title: How do I submit a Kaltura video assignment in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014146
Category: Kaltura
Short Description: How do I submit a Kaltura video assignment in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor?

Full Text:
Students in a course site have the option to submit as an assignment when they are embedding content using the Rich Content Editor. Submitting media as an assignment provides the instructor with a timestamped, non-editable copy of the original media.
How (as a student) to submit media as an assignment when embedding using the Rich Content Editor
With the Rich Content Editor open on your assignment submission page, follow the instructions in
How do I embed Kaltura media in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor?
but ensure that you designate the video as an assignment submission:
If you open the Embed Settings dialog (gear icon), you will see a Submit as Assignment choice; toggle this on before you click the </>Embed button.
If you don't open the Embed Settings dialog, but instead just click the </>Embed,  you will be asked if you want to submit as an assignment; select Yes, please.
Your content will be submitted as an assignment, which means that even if you edit the original media later, it will not change what your instructor receives as your assignment submission.

Title: How do I embed Kaltura media in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor? URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0013623 Category: Kaltura Short Description: How do I embed Kaltura media in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor? Full Text: To embed Kaltura media in Pages, Discussions, Assignments, Announcements, and anywhere else in Canvas LMS that uses the Canvas Rich Content Editor , follow the steps below. To embed Kaltura Media using the Rich Content Editor With the Rich Content Editor open, click Embed Kaltura Media. From the window that opens up on the page, if the media you want to embed is already in your My Media or a Media Gallery, click on the appropriate tab. My Media allows you to embed media from your My Media. From the Media Galleries dropdown menu, you can select a Canvas LMS site to embed media from that course's Media Gallery. Media Gallery Playlists allows you to embed Playlists that have been created in the Media Gallery of the Canvas LMS site in which you are doing the embedding. If the media you want to embed isn't already in My Media or Media Gallery or Media Gallery Playlists, you can click +Add New and add media to your My Media or Media Gallery using Media Upload, Express Capture, YouTube, or Kaltura Capture. For more help on uploading or creating media, see How do I upload existing media files to My Media? , How do I record short webcam videos using Express Capture? , How do I add YouTube videos to My Media? , or How do I get started using Kaltura Capture to record videos? If you are happy with the default embed settings: Click the </>Embed button next to the media you want to embed. The media will be embedded on the page using default settings. (Students will be asked if they want to submit as an assignment before the media is embedded; unless an instructor has directed students to do so, they can click No, thanks.) If you'd like to have more control over how your video appears: click the settings button for the media item ( gear icon to the left of the Embed button). You will then be able to select different players (e.g. whether it displays a transcript or not) and player sizes and a few other options. Please note that the skip forward 5 seconds and skip backwards 5 seconds buttons will only appear

Title: How do I embed media in Canvas LMS? URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0011407 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: How do I embed media in Canvas LMS? Full Text: If you are using Kaltura, the recommended platform for storing and sharing video in Canvas LMS, you should follow the steps in How do I Embed Kaltura media in Canvas LMS Using the Rich Content Editor? For other options: If you are an instructor, see: How do I upload and embed a media file from my computer in the Rich Content Editor as an instructor? or How do I upload and embed media from an external source in the Rich Content Editor as an instructor? For this option, you'll first need to get an embed code in order to follow the instructions in this article; you can get the embed code using one of these sets of instructions: bDrive instructions: How do I get an embed code for video stored in bDrive? YouTube instructions: Embed videos & playlists If you are a student, see: How do I upload and embed a media file from my computer in the Rich Content Editor as a student? or How do I upload and embed media from an external source in the Rich Content Editor as a student? For this option, you'll first need to get an embed code in order to follow the instructions in this article; you can get the embed code using one of these sets of instructions: bDrive instructions: How do I get an embed code for video stored in bDrive? YouTube instructions: Embed videos & playlists If you have video stored somewhere other than YouTube or bDrive, you will need to get the embed code from that environment before you proceed with the instructions. For a comparison of campus video sharing options (Canvas LMS Files, Kaltura, bDrive, and YouTube), see How should I share my video? Important Accessibility Information Instructors are encouraged to reference the Accessible Course Content Initiative website for best practices and resources for making course content accessible. In particular, you should refer to the Video Captions and Transcripts and Adding Descriptive Audio help guides to ensure that your content will be accessible to all students in your course (now and in the future). Keep in mind that it is harder and more expensive to make content accessible after-the-fact. Also, know that you are required to ensure your content is accessible if it will be made available

question: What are the steps to create a Gradescope assignment linked to Canvas LMS?
ground_truth: In Canvas LMS, create an assignment with submission type External Tool, select Gradescope, set points, and save. Configure the assignment in the embedded Gradescope UI, then sync grades back to the Canvas LMS gradebook when grading is complete.
contexts:
Title: Using the Canvas LMS (Canvas) Roster and Grade Book Sync with Gradescope
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0012021
Category: Gradescope
Short Description: Using the Canvas LMS (Canvas) Roster and Grade Book Sync with Gradescope

Full Text:
From your
Gradescope dashboard
, click + Create a New Course
Enter course information, and click Create Course
Link Gradescope course to Canvas LMS roster
From your new Gradescope course, select Course Settings
Under Canvas Course click the Link button, and select the appropriate course from Canvas LMS
Navigate to the Gradescope Course Roster, and select Sync Canvas Roster to populate you Gradescope course
Create Canvas LMS assignment and Gradescope assignment
Link Gradescope assignment to Canvas LMS assignment
From your new Gradescope assignment's Settings
Select Link underneath Canvas Assignment and select the appropriate assignment from Canvas LMS
Add Gradescope link to course navigation in Canvas LMS setting
From your course in Canvas LMS, navigate to Settings, Navigation
Find and enable the Gradescope navigation item
Sync grades to Canvas LMS grade book
When finished grading in Gradescope, navigate to Review Grades
Select Post Grades to Canvas
Refer to this
support article
(link is external)
or
video
(link is external)
for additional detail

Title: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Assignments & Grading (Instructors) URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0010793 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Assignments & Grading (Instructors) Full Text: Setting up your assignments and Gradebook are some of the most important tasks when using Canvas LMS as a tool in your class. This article for instructors provides a checklist for creating and using graded assignments, and links to specific help articles with step-by-step information that will help you set them up and use them throughout the semester. Learn about Assignments First, take a minute to read about Assignments. What is considered an Assignment? What can they be used for? Where they are kept? See this Canvas Guide for more information: What are Assignments? The Canvas LMS Assignment Workflow Once you've learned a little more about what assignments are and how they can be used, this suggested workflow can help you break down the process of setting up assignments in your course (click on the Full Screen option to view larger): 1. Create Assignment Groups Are your grades weighted? Do you have, for example, a final that is worth more than your homework assignments? If so, you may want to create Assignment Groups to organize them, as well as weight their grades. Review these Canvas Guides: How do I add an assignment group in a course? 2. Create individual assignments Allow students to submit digital files of their work using Canvas LMS. Even if you are having students turn in papers in-class, create an Assignment in Canvas LMS in order to create a column in the Gradebook where you can enter grades. Review these Canvas Guides: How do I create an assignment? How do I add or edit details in an assignment? 3. Publish assignments To publish an assignment, click the assignments Publish button, or the unpublished icon, see screenshots below. The button will change from gray to green. OR 4. Weight Assignment Groups You can weight final grades based on assignment groups. Selecting this option assigns a weight to each assignment group, not the assignments themselves. Within each assignment group, a percentage is calculated by dividing the total points a student has earned by the total points possible for all assignments in that group. How do I weight the final course grade based on assignment groups? 5. Set Your Preferred Grade Posting Policy By default, assignments have an automatic grade posting policy, meaning that grades are released to

CalCentral? These steps are the basic workflow for setting up and using Assignments and the Gradebook. Do you have more specific, detailed questions? First, please search the Canvas Instructor Guide , or our Canvas LMS Knowledge Base . If you find that you are not able to find the help article that you need, submit a Help Request form. For more on Getting Started with Canvas LMS, see these articles: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Site Setup and Management Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Communicating Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Organizing Your Course Site

question: How do I enable the Ally Course Accessibility Report in Canvas LMS?
ground_truth: Go to Settings, open the Navigation tab, find Ally Course Accessibility Report, enable it from the three-dot menu, and save. The report then appears in course navigation for instructors to review accessibility scores and guidance.
contexts:
Title: Ally Course Accessibility Report Tutorial
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014722
Category: Ally: Accessibility in Canvas LMS
Short Description: Ally Course Accessibility Report Tutorial

Full Text:
View the video version of this tutorial
The Ally Course Accessibility Report analyzes your Canvas LMS site for accessibility problems and displays results in an interactive dashboard. To enable this report, select the course where you'd like to turn on this feature. This tutorial uses the Videography 101 course.
In the course navigation, scroll down and select Settings.
At the top of the Settings page, select the Navigation link.
Locate the Ally Course Accessibility Report from the list of options. Select the three vertical dots at the end of this row and then select Enable.
After making this change, you must select the Save button on the bottom of this page.
The Ally Course Accessibility Report option now appears in the course navigation.
Selecting this option opens the Ally Course Accessibility Report Dashboard, which offers a summary and an overview of the course by highlighting (items in the below numbered list are identified in the following screenshot):
The accessibility score for the course.
A distribution of course content.
The ability to organize accessibility bugs with options to, Fix low scoring content and
The ability to organize accessibility bugs based on Content with the easiest issues to fix.
The Remaining Issues option can also be organized by Severity and by Issue
As you fix accessibility bugs, the Ally score improves.  These adjustments will make your course content more accessible for all learners.
For more information about accessibility and using this tool, contact Allyhelp@berkeley.edu

Title: As an instructor, should I include information about Ally in Canvas LMS in my syllabus?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0013287
Category: Ally: Accessibility in Canvas LMS
Short Description: As an instructor, should I include information about Ally in Canvas LMS in my syllabus?

Full Text:
Instructors are encouraged to include a statement in their course syllabus regarding Ally in Canvas LMS as a way to inform students of this important resource. Sample text includes the following:
UC Berkeley is committed to providing robust educational experiences for all learners. With this goal in mind, we activated the ALLY tool for this course. You can now download content in a format that best fits your learning preference. BeeLine Reader,  ePub, HTML,
Immersive Reader
,  MP3, and translated versions are now available for most content items. For more information, visit the alternative formats link or watch the video entitled
Ally Tutorial for Students at UC Berkeley
.

Title: Ally WYSIWYG Instructor Feedback Tool
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014719
Category: Ally: Accessibility in Canvas LMS
Short Description: Ally WYSIWYG Instructor Feedback Tool

Full Text:
View the video version of this tutorial
The Ally What You See is What You Get (or WYSIWYG) feedback tool allows Canvas LMS users to receive contextualized guidance creating accessible course materials. The tools interface guides users to identify and fix accessibility errors, so that content is accessible for a wider range of learners.
To get started, navigate to Canvas LMS content that uses the Rich Content Editor, such as a previously created Page. Select Edit.  The Ally WYSIWYG feedback indicator appears in the top right corner, providing an accessibility score.
Selecting the score icon displays the Ally interface, revealing the first accessibility error and offers assistance on how to fix it. Users have the option to select the all issues (item #1 in the below screenshot) button to review every accessibility error on the Page and evaluate how fixing each will improve your accessibility score. Select the exit button to go back to the first issue detected and begin the remediation process.
For every issue detected, users have the option to select the what this means button (item #2 in the above screenshot), review a description of those who are impacted by the accessibility error, and consider the benefits of fixing the error.
In this case, the text color used for certain headings has an insufficient contrast ratio, making it difficult for learners with visual impairments to distinguish. The Ally tool recommends higher-contrast alternatives (item #3 in the above screenshot).  By selecting one of the Ally recommended colors and choosing the apply to all occurrences option (item #4 in the above screenshot) and then selecting the Apply button (item #5 in the above screenshot), the inaccessible font colors on the entire Page are remediated to conform to Web Content Accessibility Guidelines.
As users address accessibility issues one-by-one, the accessibility score increases until all issues have been resolved and the accessibility score reaches 100 percent.

question: How can I automatically publish Course Capture recordings to my Canvas LMS site?
ground_truth: In Course Capture, open your course, edit Recording Placement, choose Publish to the Media Gallery, select your Canvas LMS site from the dropdown, and save. Recordings can publish to multiple sites if you add more than one.
contexts:
Title: How to automatically publish Course Capture recordings
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014945
Category: Course Capture
Short Description: How to automatically publish Course Capture recordings

Full Text:
Course Capture recordings can be automatically published to the Media Gallery of Canvas LMS sites and project sites. This means instructors wont need to take additional steps to share recordings, allowing members of their sites to access them more quickly!
Before you begin, ensure youve created the Canvas LMS site or project site where you want to publish.
How to create a Canvas LMS site
How to create a Project site
To enable auto-publishing
Log into the
Course Capture website
Click the course for which you want to enable auto-publishing.
Under Recording Placement, click
Edit
Choose
Publish to the Media Gallery
The dropdown will display your current semester Canvas LMS site and any project sites you have created. Select the desired site and click
Add
If you cannot find a site, you may not have the correct role in Canvas LMS. For a Canvas LMS site, these roles are Teacher, TA, Lead TA, or Designer. For a project site, these roles are Owner, or Maintainer.
Please email
coursecapture@berkeley.edu
if you would like to auto-publish into a bCourse site from previous semesters.
Repeat step 5 if you wish to add more than one site
Click
Save
These changes will update within an hour and future recordings will automatically be published. If recording has started, you will need to manually publish any older recordings.

Title: Where can I find my Course Capture recordings?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0010426
Category: Course Capture
Short Description: Where can I find my Course Capture recordings?

Full Text:
Course Capture instructors will find their Course Capture recordings within their
My Media
, which can be accessed in the global navigation (vertical navigation bar on the left) of Canvas LMS.
Instructors (or GSIs, if given
collaborator access
) will need to publish the recording into the course's
Media Gallery
to share with their students. Please see
How do I publish media from My Media to a Media Gallery in Canvas LMS?
for instructions. You may also choose to
set Course Capture to automatically publish
into a course Media Gallery.
Students will only be able to access Course Capture recordings in Media Gallery if the video has been published.

Course Captures in a Canvas LMS Project Site You can share videos using a Canvas LMS Project Site that you only add specific students to. This setup works particularly well if you would like to regularly share Course Capture videos with a group of specific students, with the option of auto-publishing only to these students. Follow the instructions in How do I create a Project Site? to set up your project site. Follow the instructions in How do I add users to my course site? to add your students to the site. Your students should see the project site on their Canvas LMS courses list. You can also send them the link to the site to remind them where to find the videos. You may publish media to your new project site's media gallery following the instructions in How do I publish media from My Media to a Media Gallery in Canvas LMS? . These videos will only be shared with students added to the project site. If you would like to enable auto-publishing of Course Capture videos to this project site, please follow the instructions at How to automatically publish Course Capture recordings .

question: How do I submit an assignment in Canvas LMS?
ground_truth: Open the assignment, click Submit Assignment, choose the required submission type (file upload, text entry, URL, or media), add your work, and submit. Confirm the success message and timestamp.
contexts:
Title: How do I submit a Kaltura video assignment in Canvas LMS using the Rich Content Editor?

Title: Issue submitting an Assignment in Canvas LMS
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0012198
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: Issue submitting an Assignment in Canvas LMS

Full Text:
If you experience technical problems while submitting an Assignment in Canvas LMS, take these steps to troubleshoot the issue.
Make sure you've updated to the latest browser version
https://www.whatismybrowser.com/
Clear your browser cache
https://clear-my-cache.com/
Disable browser add-ons/extensions
Some privacy or ad-blockers have been known to cause issues
Try submitting your assignment using a private/incognito window. Instructions for commonly used browsers:
Google Chrome
Mozilla Firefox
Safari
Microsoft Edge
Try a different browser. Repeat.
If you continue to experience an issue, continue.
Take a full-page screenshot
https://www.take-a-screenshot.org/
Send the screenshot, a description of problem, and a link to the Assignment to the Canvas LMS Help team
canvas lmshelp@berkeley.edu
Related
Canvas Guide:
What are the browser and computer requirements for Canvas?

question: What is SuiteC and what tools does it include?
ground_truth: SuiteC is a set of engagement tools in Canvas LMS: Asset Library for sharing course assets, Whiteboards for collaborative visual work, and Engagement Index for participation tracking. Instructors enable individual tools from course navigation settings.
contexts:
Title: How do I manage Course Navigation links in my Canvas LMS Site?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014265
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: How do I manage Course Navigation links in my Canvas LMS Site?

Full Text:
As an instructor, you can add or remove links to the course navigation that appears on the left side of your Canvas LMS site. Adding relevant links to your Course Navigation keeps your course site organized for students accessing online resources and tools.
For instructions on how to manage course Navigation Links, please see this Canvas guide:
How do I manage Course Navigation Links?
What links can I add to my Course Navigation?
Several optional tools are available to course sites but are hidden by default. These include:
Course Captures
Library Guide
Mailing List
Gradescope
Ally Instructor Report
Zoom
Course Evaluations
Asset Library (SuiteC)
Engagement Index (SuiteC).
Other external apps and websites can also be added to the course navigation menu. For more information, please see our related help articles:
Adding an external app
Linking to an external website

Title: How do I enable the SuiteC tools in Canvas LMS? URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0011373 Category: SuiteC (formerly Collabosphere) Short Description: How do I enable the SuiteC tools in Canvas LMS? Full Text: Note: SuiteC is no longer in active development. It will enter a read-only mode on August 20, 2025. It will be permanently retired on June 30, 2026. Please see the SuiteC Retirement FAQ for more information. The SuiteC tools (Asset Library, Whiteboards, Engagement Index, and Impact Studio) are available for use in Canvas LMS sites. Please create a site first (instructions: Course Site or Project Site *), and then enable the tool(s). * See FAQ below STEP 1 From your course site, click on Settings in the Course Navigation menu. STEP 2 (1) Click on the Navigation tab. (2) The links to the Asset Library, Engagement Index, and Whiteboards are located in the hidden links table, beneath the table of enabled links. The Impact Studio is currently available by request. If you would like to use this tool, send an email to canvas lmshelp@berkeley.edu along with the URL of your Canvas LMS site. STEP 3 (1) Find the SuiteC tool(s) that you would like to use. (2) Enable a tool by clicking on the gear icon for each, and selecting 'Enable'. (3) Make sure to click 'Save'. SUITEC TOOLS ENABLED Once you Save the changes, the browser will refresh and you will see the links to the tools in your Course Navigation menu. FAQs Why can't I see the SuiteC tools in the Navigation settings (Step 3)? This is likely because you are using a Project Site, and SuiteC is only available for official/unofficial courses. Please contact our help desk canvas lmshelp@berkeley.edu and provide them with a link to your Project Site. They can make it into an unofficial course, which will allow you to have access to and use the SuiteC tools. Do the SuiteC tools work on mobile? The SuiteC tools will not work when using the Canvas App on a mobile device. However, there is some, albeit limited, functionality when using the browser on your mobile device, which varies depending on the mobile platform and browser. For optimal performance of these tools, it is best to use a laptop or desktop with a stable internet connection. I only want to use one of the tools. Do I have to enable both tools in order for one of them to work? You do

SuiteC is no longer in active development. It will enter a read-only mode on August 20, 2025. It will be permanently retired on June 30, 2026. Please see the
SuiteC Retirement FAQ
for more information.

question: Why is my Canvas LMS quiz not showing up for students?
ground_truth: Check whether the quiz is unpublished, outside its Available From or Until dates, inside a locked module, or limited to specific sections. Use Student View to see what learners see.
contexts: and provide them with an alternate Available Until date and time. If you have a significant number of students with accommodations, you may want to create a custom section for students to be able to quickly assign extended time or additional attempts rather than assigning them individually. Grant extended time and/or additional attempts for students. Publish the quiz and use the Moderate feature to give extra time to a specific student or multiple students. Follow the instructions below depending on if youre using Classic Quizzes or New Quizzes. Classic Quizzes: Accommodations need to be set up separately in every quiz for the student(s). Select the Moderate This Quiz link on the right side of the Quiz page. Then, follow this Canvas Guide to grant extra time or this Canvas Guide to grant extra attempts for a student or group of students. Note that the Extra time on every attempt field only displays for quizzes that have a set time limit. New Quizzes: Accommodations that you set for one quiz in your course will apply to all quizzes in that course for the student(s) — so you only need to set it up once per course. Open the Build page for any New Quiz, select the Moderate tab, and follow this Canvas Guide to make time adjustments or this Canvas Guide to grant extra attempts for a specific student. Explain to students how they receive their extended time or attempts. When students first click on the quiz, it will not be obvious that the time has been extended or that they have multiple attempts. You can add an explanation by editing the quiz instructions and providing the information. Here is an example of the instructions you can use: You can start this quiz anytime between [availability start date/time] and [availability end date/time] by clicking Take the Quiz below. Once you start this quiz, you will have the amount of time indicated as Time Limit to complete your quiz. Make sure to start in enough time so that you will be finished by [availability end date/time] . If you are still taking the quiz at [availability end date/time] , the quiz will auto-submit even if you have not taken the entire amount of time you have been given! Extending Deadlines for Assignments Extended due dates on assignments is a common student accommodation. There are two ways to extend due dates on assignments

Title: Unable to create a site/site does not show on Canvas LMS
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014733
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: Unable to create a site/site does not show on Canvas LMS

Full Text:
To determine if you have the ability to create or edit a Canvas LMS site, check the online schedule of classes:
Go to
https://classes.berkeley.edu/
Locate the specific course you are teaching.
Verify that your name is listed as the Instructor of Record.
If your name is not displayed, it indicates that the system does not recognize you as the designated individual with access to create the site.
To address this issue, it is advisable to communicate with your department scheduler to ensure that you are listed as the Instructor of Record.

Title: I just added the course but I can't log into Canvas LMS
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0012161
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: I just added the course but I can't log into Canvas LMS

Full Text:
Canvas LMS enrollments are refreshed daily from the Student Information System at around 6AM. Therefore, students who just registered for the course on CalCentral, or instructors who have just been added by their department scheduler as the official instructor of a course, will get access to the course site on the following day.
If needed, anyone with the Teacher role in the course site can give immediate access by manually adding students or other instructors. Please see
How do I add users to my course site?
for instructions on how to add people to a course site.

question: How do I set up academic integrity tools in Canvas LMS assignments?
ground_truth: Create or edit an online assignment, enable file or text submission, open Plagiarism Review, select a tool such as Turnitin, configure originality report settings, and save. Inform students that submissions will be checked.
contexts: Title: How to Access and Complete your Academic Integrity Survey Assignment URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014639 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: How to Access and Complete your Academic Integrity Survey Assignment Full Text: Beginning in Fall 2023, all students will be required to demonstrate their participation in each course to satisfy federal requirements. Learn more about Financial Aid eligibility . Students will demonstrate their participation by completing a survey assignment that will be administered via Canvas LMS, or via email (if your course is not using Canvas LMS). The assignment will be made available in the first weeks of the term. How To Access Your Assignment(s) 1. From Canvas LMS There are two ways to access the assignment from Canvas LMS: A. Select the Academic Integrity tab in your courses navigation menu and then select the survey displayed under Current Surveys: *If the survey does not open in a new window/tab, you might try accessing the link from a private/incognito browser window, or using an alternate web browser. B. From Assignments find the assignment named Academic Integrity Assignment… 2. From email (If your course is not using Canvas LMS) Look for an email from courseparticipation@berkeley.edu with the subject Course Participation Assignment and open the Confirm your participation now link. 3. From surveys.berkeley.edu You can also access your surveys directly from surveys.berkeley.edu Completing the Assignment Regardless of how you access the assignment (Canvas LMS or email) it will look the same. Preview of actual assignment . To complete the assignment: read the text, check the box, and select submit Frequently Asked Questions Q: Do I have to complete a survey for each of my courses? A: Yes, federal requirements require us to verify student participation for every enrollment. Q: Why cant I find an Academic Integrity assignment in one of my Canvas LMS sites? A: This assignment may not exist in a Canvas LMS site if it is associated with an ungraded/secondary section. A: The instructor may have opted out of the Canvas LMS assignment, in which case you will receive the assignment via email. A: The assignment is only available to officially enrolled students, not auditors or others enrolled outside of CalCentral. A: If you recently enrolled in the course, it may take up to two business days to access the assignment. You can view all of your survey assignments at surveys.berkeley.edu. Q: I completed one of my surveys, but when I view it in Canvas LMS, it says No submission. Why is this?

Title: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Assignments & Grading (Instructors) URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0010793 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Assignments & Grading (Instructors) Full Text: Setting up your assignments and Gradebook are some of the most important tasks when using Canvas LMS as a tool in your class. This article for instructors provides a checklist for creating and using graded assignments, and links to specific help articles with step-by-step information that will help you set them up and use them throughout the semester. Learn about Assignments First, take a minute to read about Assignments. What is considered an Assignment? What can they be used for? Where they are kept? See this Canvas Guide for more information: What are Assignments? The Canvas LMS Assignment Workflow Once you've learned a little more about what assignments are and how they can be used, this suggested workflow can help you break down the process of setting up assignments in your course (click on the Full Screen option to view larger): 1. Create Assignment Groups Are your grades weighted? Do you have, for example, a final that is worth more than your homework assignments? If so, you may want to create Assignment Groups to organize them, as well as weight their grades. Review these Canvas Guides: How do I add an assignment group in a course? 2. Create individual assignments Allow students to submit digital files of their work using Canvas LMS. Even if you are having students turn in papers in-class, create an Assignment in Canvas LMS in order to create a column in the Gradebook where you can enter grades. Review these Canvas Guides: How do I create an assignment? How do I add or edit details in an assignment? 3. Publish assignments To publish an assignment, click the assignments Publish button, or the unpublished icon, see screenshots below. The button will change from gray to green. OR 4. Weight Assignment Groups You can weight final grades based on assignment groups. Selecting this option assigns a weight to each assignment group, not the assignments themselves. Within each assignment group, a percentage is calculated by dividing the total points a student has earned by the total points possible for all assignments in that group. How do I weight the final course grade based on assignment groups? 5. Set Your Preferred Grade Posting Policy By default, assignments have an automatic grade posting policy, meaning that grades are released to

Title: Academic Participation Canvas LMS Assignment URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0014634 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: Academic Participation Canvas LMS Assignment Full Text: Due to a federal mandate, we must demonstrate student participation in all courses. In order to comply with this requirement, we will create an ungraded assignment in each Canvas LMS site. A global announcement for students will be posted on the Canvas LMS dashboard with information about the assignment. We hope that instructors will find this addition unobtrusive and straightforward. We understand that in certain cases, instructors would prefer to opt out, so that an assignment is not created in their Canvas LMS site. If you choose to opt out following the instructions below, your students will receive the same survey via email. If a course does not have a published Canvas LMS site at the beginning of the term, it will also receive the survey via email. Student View of the Canvas LMS assignment: Because the survey is only assigned to students, instructors, GSIs, and anyone not officially registered will not be able to view or access the survey within Canvas LMS. For reference, the survey will appear as below in Canvas LMS. Also see this preview of the actual assignment . Gradebook View: While the assignment does not factor into the final grade, it will appear as a column in the course gradebook with a value of 1 point visible to students and instructors. If your course is set to use a manual grade posting policy by default, this assignment will follow that rule, and students will not be able to verify that they have completed the assignment. We recommend setting an automatic grade posting policy for this specific assignment (which will not impact your ability to use a manual grade posting policy for all other assignments). Assignment View The assignment will be added to a new, separate assignment group at the bottom of your course's Assignments page. To opt out of the Canvas LMS assignment: Instructors will have several days to opt out. You will receive an email from courseparticipation@berkeley.edu with the exact opt-out window for the term. Use the link in the email you received from courseparticipation@berkeley.edu, or navigate to surveys.berkeley.edu Select the course you wish to opt out Select Manage Settings Toggle the Opt in to this survey setting from Yes to No Select Update Repeat for other desired courses, if necessary To opt back in to the Canvas LMS Assignment: You can change your mind and

question: How do I add a Zoom meeting to a Canvas LMS calendar?
ground_truth: Use the Canvas LMS Zoom integration to schedule a meeting so it appears on the course calendar. Add the meeting from the course Zoom tool or calendar, set date and time, and save so students can join from the course site.
contexts:
Title: Canvas LMS Zoom Integration
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0013479
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: Canvas LMS Zoom Integration

Full Text:
Canvas LMS Zoom Integration
All Canvas LMS course sites have a Zoom integration that can be enabled. The integration allows instructors to:
Schedule meetings
Start meetings
View upcoming meetings
View previous meetings and recordings*
*Note that recordings stored in Zoom are temporary and will be automatically deleted
per campus policy
. For long-term storage of zoom recordings, we recommend using Kaltura:
How can I share my Zoom Recordings in Canvas LMS using Kaltura?
Enabling the integration:
This integration is disabled by default. To enable it, go to your course Settings page, select the navigation tab, find the Zoom menu option and click the three vertical dot icon (1) select enable (2) and then save (3).
Using the Integration /
Scheduling a Meeting
Scheduling a meeting within the integration has the same interface as scheduling a meeting directly through berkeley.zoom.us
Meetings scheduled through the integration will automatically appear on student's calendars, and also send a notification message to the student's inbox.
Note that only meetings scheduled via the integration (or manually imported into it) will appear there. To view all of your meetings, log in to berkeley.zoom.us
Student Access
Students can join a Zoom meeting scheduled using the integration:
Through the Zoom menu item in the course navigation
Via the event on their Canvas LMS calendar
Via the link included in the email notification that is sent at the time that a meeting is scheduled
Importing Meetings
If you've scheduled a meeting outside of the integration and would like to make it visible to your students, you can use the Import Meeting function.
To import a meeting, select the three vertical dot menu in the top-right corner of the integration, select import meeting and paste the meeting ID.
Limitations
The Canvas LMS-Zoom integration uses email address as an identifier. If a user does not have an @berkeley.edu email address, they will not be able to access the integration. Keep this in mind, as Calnet Sponsored Guests will need to be granted separate access.

Title: How can I share my Zoom Recordings in Canvas LMS using Kaltura? URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0013637 Category: Kaltura Short Description: How can I share my Zoom Recordings in Canvas LMS using Kaltura? Full Text: Important Student Privacy Information If you intend to share video that includes student participation (identifying information such as name, image, or voice), you must follow certain practices; please see the Academic Senate FAQ If I record a class lecture or discussion, and it includes student participation (identifying information such as name, image, or voice), can I share the recording with individuals who are not enrolled in the course? For most Zoom Cloud recordings You will need to: Wait until the recordings appear in your My Media . (Your recordings are automatically copied from the Zoom Cloud to your Kaltura My Media, assuming you have a Kaltura account; if you do not have a Kaltura account, see instructions below.) Recordings should appear shortly after you stop recording in Zoom; this can sometimes be minutes but at other times may take several hours. Share the recordings. You have a variety of ways to do this, see How do I share media in Kaltura within Canvas LMS? Other things you may want to know how to do: To get to your My Media , log into Canvas LMS and find My Media in the global navigation (vertical navigation bar on the left) of Canvas LMS. You can also access My Media through MediaSpace . To ensure you have a Kaltura account (which you must have when your Zoom recordings are created), see How can I be sure that I have a Kaltura account? To ensure that your recordings are Zoom Cloud recordings , see How do I enable Zoom Cloud recordings? (Please note: Zoom Cloud Recordings will be turned off by default on January 6, 2021. If you previously used Cloud recordings, you will need to re-enable cloud recordings in Zoom.) For Zoom Cloud recordings created prior to having a Kaltura account You will need to: Download these recordings to your hard disk. (For more info on downloading these recordings, follow the instructions in How do I download my Zoom Cloud recordings? ) Upload the media to Kaltura; see How do I upload media to My Media? Share the recordings. You have a variety of ways to do this, all documented in How do I share media in Kaltura within Canvas LMS? For Zoom recordings that

Title: How do I add my Canvas LMS Calendar to bCal?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0010692
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: How do I add my Canvas LMS Calendar to bCal?

Full Text:
You can import a feed of your Canvas LMS Calendar into bCal, which is helpful if you use bCal for to keep track of important events and/or due dates. Any changes that you make to events in Canvas LMS will be updated in bCal.
STEP 1
Log into Canvas LMS and click
Calendar
at the top navigation menu.
STEP 2
Click
Calendar Feed
on the right side under the calendar in Canvas LMS.
STEP 3
Copy the calendar feed link from the pop-up menu.
STEP 4
Go to
bCal
-
http://bcal.berkeley.edu/
STEP 5
In the drop-down menu next to
[1] Other Calendars
, select
[2] Add by URL
FINAL STEP
Paste the feed link that you copied in
Step 3
into the pop-up box  and click Add Calendar. Your Canvas LMS Calendar has been added to bCal!

question: How do I require students to move through modules in order?
ground_truth: In Modules, enable requirement settings so students must complete items in sequence. Set prerequisites or require students to move through modules in order before accessing later module content.
contexts: Title: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Organizing Your Course Site URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0010792 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: Getting Started with Canvas LMS: Organizing Your Course Site Full Text: Designing your course site can involve a number of ways of organizing your materials and customizing the way that users navigate the site and access those materials. In this article for instructors, we list some of the ways that you can change your course site to create an online space that is easy for you and your students to use and find content. Modules Use this tool to add and organize all of your various course materials and present them to students. Any files that you upload into Modules also get automatically saved in Files. You can move content more freely and add virtually any type of content that you would like. Customize the Course Navigation menu The Course Navigation Menu is a series of links on the left side of your course site that help you and your students access its various areas and/or tools. You cant rename these links. Links that look grayed out mean that there is no content in those areas/tools, and that they are hidden from students. See this Canvas Guide for instructions on how you can re-order and/or hide links on the Course Navigation menu: How do I use the Course Navigation Menu as an instructor? Set the course home page By default, the Home page of any Canvas LMS course site is a space called the Course Activity Stream which lists recent activity in the course. You can change this so that everyone in your course sees the content that you deem is the most important as soon as they enter the site. See this Canvas Guide for instructions: How do I change the Course Home Page? Use external tools Many popular external tools such as Gradescope, Ed Discussions, and Zoom are installed in all courses and can be enabled by adding them to the navigation menu following the steps above. If you wish to add a different tool, you can enable these apps, and many others, from the App Center, which you can find by going to the Settings link on the Course Navigation menu, and then clicking on the Apps tab. The instructions for adding external tools vary for each app. Some require specific numerical keys and codes that are provided by the vendor. Please contact the

Title: How can I organize my Canvas LMS discussion forums?
URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0012039
Category: Canvas LMS
Short Description: How can I organize my Canvas LMS discussion forums?

Full Text:
By default, discussion forums are arranged by recent activity. If you would like to manually order the forums to a specific order, you will have to first pin the forum and then you will be able to click-and-drag them in the order you prefer.
Step-by-step instructions are available in this Canvas Guide:
How do I pin a discussion in a course?

Title: What options are available for setting up my multiple-section course on Canvas LMS? URL: https://berkeley.service-now.com/kb_view.do?sysparm_article=KB0012108 Category: Canvas LMS Short Description: What options are available for setting up my multiple-section course on Canvas LMS? Full Text: If you are teaching a course with multiple sections, we recommend a couple of different options for setting up your Canvas LMS sites for them, depending on how you and your teaching team would like to manage your course materials and student enrollments. Option A: One site for all sections (Recommended for most situations) In this set up, there is one site that has all of the rosters, as well as the course materials and activities/assessments (e.g. Files, Announcements, Discussion Forums, Assignments, etc.). Many instructors of multiple-section courses use this method. Advantages One site for all students, course materials, and grades -- which means potentially less confusion from students about where course materials and activities are than if there were multiple course sites for each section. Consistency in access and availability of resources FAQs about this setup Q: Will I be able to filter to a particular section, so that I don't have to look through the entire roster of students to find the ones in my section? A: Yes! You can filter to a section in the Gradebook, Speedgrader, as well as in the Inbox tool, see screenshots below: Section filter in the Gradebook Section filter in the Speedgrader Section filter in the Inbox (Canvas LMS email tool) Q: Why do I need to include the DIS/LAB rosters in this site if the LEC roster will have all of the students? A: Including the DIS/LAB rosters in the site in addition to the LEC roster allows instructors to do the following: Filter the Gradebook by section Create Differentiated Assignments by section (assignments that are only assigned to a specific section) Post Announcements that are only visible to a specific section Create Discussions that are only visible to a specific section Send Inbox/Conversation messages to just a specific section Including all section rosters will ensure that students who are not enrolled in the LEC or a DIS/LAB section will have access to the course site. Q: Since all students have access to this site, can I restrict access to certain files by section so that students from other sections dont see them? A: No, files or folders in the Files tool cannot be restricted to a particular section of
